name: Turborepo
what_it_does: High-performance build system for JavaScript/TypeScript monorepos. Understands the dependency graph between packages in a monorepo, caches task outputs locally and remotely, and parallelizes tasks intelligently. Remote cache can be hosted on Vercel or self-hosted. Provides CLI and a limited remote cache REST API.
use_cases: Accelerating monorepo CI builds with remote task output caching
Parallelizing build, test, and lint tasks across packages based on dependency order
Sharing build cache between local developer machines and CI
Filtering tasks to only affected packages in a monorepo
Generating monorepo scaffolding and task pipelines
not_for: Non-JavaScript/TypeScript monorepos (Nx or Bazel are better for polyglot)
Teams that need fine-grained task graph visualization or Nx-level plugin ecosystem
Self-hosted remote cache without infrastructure budget
best_when: An agent is managing a JavaScript/TypeScript monorepo and needs to intelligently run only affected tasks with remote caching to speed up CI.
avoid_when: You have a single package project, a polyglot monorepo, or need complex dependency injection that Nx handles better.

auth.notes: Vercel Remote Cache uses Vercel API tokens (TURBO_TOKEN). Self-hosted remote cache implementations vary. Token is set via TURBO_TOKEN env variable and TURBO_TEAM for Vercel team-scoped caching.

pricing.free_tier_limits: OSS CLI is fully free. Vercel Remote Cache is free on Vercel (included with Vercel account, which has its own tiers).
pricing.paid_tiers: Self-hosted remote cache requires infrastructure. Vercel team plans from $20/user/mo cover remote cache usage.
pricing.notes: Core OSS tool is free with no restrictions. Remote cache via Vercel is effectively free if already on Vercel. Self-hosted options (ducktape, turborepo-remote-cache open source) are available for non-Vercel users.

performance.rate_limits: N/A - local tool (remote cache API has Vercel platform limits)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Turborepo CLI output is well-formatted with colored task summaries showing hit/miss cache status, task duration, and failure context. Errors propagate from underlying tasks (tsc, jest, etc.) so root cause clarity depends on those tools. Cache miss reasons are not always surfaced.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Task outputs are content-hashed and cached deterministically. Running the same task twice on unchanged inputs returns cached results. Remote cache reads are idempotent. Cache writes are deduplicated by hash.
known_agent_gotchas: TURBO_TOKEN and TURBO_TEAM must both be set for Vercel remote cache - missing either silently falls back to local cache
turbo.json pipeline configuration syntax changed between v1 and v2 (tasks vs pipeline key)
The --filter flag for affected packages requires a valid git history; shallow clones in CI can produce unexpected results
turbo prune for Docker deployments requires careful configuration to avoid including unnecessary packages
Cache invalidation happens on any input change including package.json changes from unrelated dependency updates
Turborepo does not manage package manager (npm/yarn/pnpm) workspace configuration - agents must handle both separately

score_rationale: Turborepo is a focused, well-documented tool that solves a specific problem (monorepo task caching) exceptionally well. Agent-readiness is above average for a local CLI tool because the configuration is declarative JSON, the CLI output is structured and parseable, and the remote cache API adds a hosted component. Scores are limited by the lack of a comprehensive REST API beyond cache storage, dependency on Vercel for the best remote cache experience, and the fact that git history depth in CI environments can cause surprising behavior with the --filter flag.
